summarization.a: Spectra of X-ray absorption near-edge structure (XANES) for various Li-MnO2 compositions, highlighting the changes in manganese oxidation states.

data_extraction.a:
| Sample         | Mn⁴⁺/Mn³⁺ Ratio | Energy Range (eV) | Key Features |
|---------------|-----------------|--------------------|--------------|
| MnO₂          | 100/0          | 638–650           | Strong Mn⁴⁺ peak near 644 eV |
| 10cLi-MnO₂    | ~100/0         | 638–650           | Similar to MnO₂, slight broadening |
| 50cLi-MnO₂    | 80/20          | 638–650           | Mn³⁺ peak emerges near 642 eV |
| 100cLi-MnO₂   | ~60/40         | 638–650           | Mn³⁺ peak more pronounced |
| 200cLi-MnO₂   | Lower Mn⁴⁺     | 638–650           | Mn³⁺ dominant, Mn⁴⁺ reduced |

a.question: What are the reference lines made of?
a.answer: The reference lines are  linear combinations of Mn4+ and Mn3+

a.question: Does the MnO2 sample resemble the Mn4+ reference?
What is the energy of Mn3+ and Mn4+?
a.answer:
In the Mn LIII-edge XANES 
1. Mn 4+ has energy of 644 eV
2. Mn 3+ has energy of 641 eV

a.question: What can be said about the behaviour of Li-MnO2?
a.answer:
The 10cLi−MnO2 and 50cLi−MnO2 samples resemble the reference. The 100cLi−MnO2 and 200cLi−MnO2 samples match the reference calculated
with the Mn4+/Mn3+ ratio of 80%/20%. The 300cLi−MnO2 sample resembles mostly the reference where the ratio of Mn4+/Mn3+ is 60%/40%, which is in line with the expectation for this sample with the almost stoichiometric composition of Li1.1Mn2O4.
